purpose: First source-bound extraction layer. These records quote or paraphrase exact paper claims and bind them to rows/check targets. They are not proof results.

claims.claim_text: Cosmohedra are polytopes whose faces capture Russian-doll / non-overlapping subpolygon structures determining the wavefunction; they are related to associahedra by blowing up faces and have facet inequalities that further shave the associahedron.

The cosmohedron is obtained by imposing additional inequalities over the associahedron: for each partial triangulation / collection of non-overlapping chords C, sum_{I subset C} X_I >= sum delta_P, with delta_P positive and satisfying delta_P + delta_P' <= delta_{P union P'} + delta_{P intersection P'}, with delta_full = 0.
The paper proves the correctness of the cosmohedron construction. The face lattice of the (n-1)-cosmohedron is anti-isomorphic to the poset of Matryoshkas of an (n+2)-gon.

For bi-adjoint phi^3 theory, the scattering form is directly connected to the kinematic associahedron; the tree amplitude is the canonical form of this positive geometry, and locality/unitarity/factorization follow from the associahedron geometry.

The amplituhedron admits fundamentally combinatorial/topological characterizations: projected data have maximal winding number, equivalently canonical one-dimensional projections have a maximal number of sign flips; locality and unitarity follow as consequences of this binary code.

The curve-integral formalism defines u_C variables for curves C on a surface satisfying u_C + product_D u_D^{#int(C,D)} = 1; in the positive region this gives binary behavior, and curve integrals use these variables to produce amplitudes and factorization behavior.

claims.source_anchor: Introduction and Summary, kinematic shift equations
claims.claim_text: Stringy Tr(phi^3) amplitudes determine scaffolded-gluon / Yang-Mills amplitudes by a kinematic shift: Xe,e -> Xe,e + 1/alpha', Xo,o -> Xo,o - 1/alpha', Xe,o unchanged; this changes the measure from product dy_i/y_i to product dy_i/y_i^2 in a scaffolding triangulation.

Amplituhedron-Prime has the same boundary structure and logarithmic form as the Amplituhedron but differs in the bulk as a geometric space; this is a caution against identifying objects merely by shared boundary/log-form data.
